name: Code Health Venture
description: Audits any GitHub repository for security vulnerabilities, outdated dependencies, and code quality issues. Generates pre-filled GitHub issue links for one-click issue creation.

auditScope.description: What to audit: security vulns, outdated deps, code quality, or all

fields.name: issues
fields.description: Array of issues found, each with title, severity, description, createIssueUrl, and reasoning

fields.name: issueCount
fields.description: Counts by severity: { critical, high, medium, low }

invariants.invariant: Audit repository {{repoUrl}} on branch {{branch}}. Scope: {{auditScope}}. PHASE 1: EXPLORE & REASON. Before generating any output, you MUST create an artifact 'audit-reasoning.md' documenting your search strategy, raw findings, and filtering logic.
invariants.measurement: Verify 'audit-reasoning.md' artifact exists before any issues are reported in output.

do: Step 1: Create audit-reasoning.md. Step 2: List discovered files. Step 3: Analyze each for issues.
dont: Skip the reasoning phase and jump straight to the final JSON output.

invariants.commentary: Forcing a reasoning phase drastically improves the quality and accuracy of the final audit.

invariants.invariant: For each issue found, generate a pre-filled GitHub issue creation URL. Format: https://github.com/OWNER/REPO/issues/new?title=TITLE&body=BODY&labels=LABELS. URL-encode title and body. Group related minor issues into single 'Cluster' issues to avoid spamming.
invariants.measurement: Count minor issues. If >5 similar issues exist, verify they are grouped into cluster issues. Each issue must have valid createIssueUrl.

do: Group 5 missing return types into one 'Fix Missing Return Types in /src' issue.
dont: Create 5 separate issues for the same minor style violation.

invariants.commentary: Cluster issues prevent notification fatigue. Pre-filled URLs enable one-click action.

invariants.invariant: PRIORITIZE: Security (Critical > High) > Correctness (Bugs) > Dependencies > Quality. Report security vulnerabilities >= {{securityThreshold}} immediately. Include CVE ID, CVSS, and specific remediation.
invariants.measurement: Check output order: Security issues must appear before Quality issues in the issues array.

do: Rank a CVSS 9.8 SQL Injection above a missing JSDoc comment.
dont: Bury a critical security vulnerability under a list of style nits.

invariants.commentary: Critical issues must not be lost in the noise. Strict prioritization ensures value.

invariants.invariant: When auditing dependencies, check: outdated packages, known vulnerabilities (CVEs), unused dependencies, and lockfile consistency. Cross-reference version numbers against the official registry.
invariants.measurement: For each dependency issue, verify external source was checked (npm registry, CVE database).

do: Verify 'react' version in package.json against npm registry latest.
dont: Assume package versions are current without checking external data.

invariants.commentary: Dependency auditing requires external validation, not just static file analysis.

invariants.invariant: When auditing quality, look for: logic errors, missing tests, poor documentation, and violations of project standards (CONTRIBUTING.md/AGENTS.md). IGNORE: dist/, node_modules/, vendor/, and auto-generated files.
invariants.measurement: Confirm no issues reference files in dist/, node_modules/, vendor/, or .min.js files.

do: Analyze src/lib/api.ts for error handling gaps.
dont: Report lint errors in minified files under dist/ or third-party code in vendor/.

invariants.commentary: Focus on the code the developer writes and maintains. Ignore build artifacts.

invariants.invariant: Calculate Health Score (0-100): Start at 100. Deduct: Critical Vuln (-25), High Vuln (-10), Bug (-5), Outdated Dep (-2), Quality Nit (-1). Floor at 0.
invariants.measurement: Verify healthScore = max(0, 100 - (criticals×25 + highs×10 + bugs×5 + deps×2 + nits×1)).

dont: Assign arbitrary scores like '80 because it looks okay'.

invariants.commentary: A deterministic scoring rubric ensures consistency across different runs and repos.

invariants.invariant: Output JSON 'issues' array must include: title, severity, description, createIssueUrl, and 'reasoning' (why this matters).
invariants.measurement: For each issue in output, verify all 5 required fields are present and non-empty.

do: Item: { ... reasoning: 'SQL injection allows attackers to dump the database.' }
dont: Omit the 'reasoning' field, leaving the user to guess why an issue is important.

invariants.commentary: Explaining 'why' educates the user and justifies the issue report.
